fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 70 is inverted: `if not clamp` should be `if clamp`, causing clamping to be applied when clamp=False and skipped when clamp=True.

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the clamping block should execute; the condition `if not clamp` inverts this logic so clamping is skipped when requested.
The then-branch of the if-not-clamp block should only execute when clamp is False (extrapolation allowed), not when clamp is True.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: When clamp=True the final y value must lie within [min(y0,y1), max(y0,y1)]; the inverted condition causes this invariant to be violated.

spec.expr: (not clamp) or (y__5 >= min(y0, y1) and y__5 <= max(y0, y1))

constraints.intent: Before returning, if clamp=True the value should already be bounded; the bug causes y__5 to be unclamped when clamp=True.
